concerns: The database migration script (V1__create_initial_schema.sql) does not include database comments (COMMENT ON TABLE/COLUMN) for table or column-level metadata, which is recommended for data catalog standards.
The application name in LeadGenApplication is generic and should be verified for consistency across the modular structure.
The main application.yml file configures an in-memory H2 database as the default datasource instead of a PostgreSQL configuration for production, which deviates from the tech spec requirements.
The database migration script (V1__create_campaigns_and_leads.sql) lacks database comments (COMMENT ON TABLE/COLUMN) for table or column documentation.

The pom.xml only imports 'spring-boot-starter-jdbc' instead of 'spring-boot-starter-data-jpa', which is different from the other PRs and might require manual mapping if Spring Data JPA is needed later.
Tests are directly writing SQL strings in JdbcTemplate which could be modernized or extracted if standard Spring Data repositories are introduced later.
